name: epicscorelibs

metadata.name: epicscorelibs
metadata.summary: The EPICS Core libraries for use by python modules

documentation:
The EPICS (Experimental Physics and Industrial Control System) core libraries
for use by python modules.  Either dynamically with ctypes or statically by compiled extension.

modules.name: python.epicscorelibs.path.cothread
documentation:
I automatically configure for use with cothread.catools

eg. attempt to use libca from this packages if available.

    try:
        import epicscorelibs.path.cothread
    except ImportError:
        pass
    from cothread.catools import caget
    ...

modules.name: python.epicscorelibs.path.pyepics
documentation:
I automatically configure for use with pyepics

eg. attempt to use libca from this packages if available.

    try:
        import epicscorelibs.path.pyepics
    except ImportError:
        pass
    import epics
    ...

functions.name: python.epicscorelibs.path.get_lib
documentation:
Fetch library path by name.

:param str name: A library name.  eg. 'Com' or 'ca'.
:returns: The full absolute path to this library.

modules.name: python.epicscorelibs.ca.py23
documentation: Some simple python2/python3 compatibility tricks.
Some of this is drastically reduced from six.py

modules.name: python.epicscorelibs
documentation:
This package holds the non-python libraries which consitute EPICS Base.

https://epics.anl.gov/

See epicscorelibs.license for terms

modules.name: python.epicscorelibs.ioc
documentation: Minimal softIoc

functions.name: python.epicscorelibs.ioc.ioc
documentation: Execute IOC shell command

modules.name: python.epicscorelibs.version
documentation:
Version information for the epicscorelibs package.

Version numbers are encoded as:

BASEVERSION.99.ABI.SRC

functions.name: python.epicscorelibs.version.abi_requires
documentation:
Return a version requirement string which identifies
a range of version which will be ABI compatible with this one.
For use by modules with non-python dependencies on our libraries.

eg. "epicscorelibs >=7.0.1.99.1.1a5, <7.0.1.99.2"

variables.name: python.epicscorelibs.version.version
documentation: as a string

modules.name: python.epicscorelibs.ca.cadef
documentation:
Functions imported directly from libca.

See http://www.aps.anl.gov/epics/base/R3-14/11-docs/CAref.html for detailed
documentation of the functions below.

This module is a thin wrapper over the cadef.h file to be found in
    $EPICS_BASE/include/cadef.h

functions.name: python.epicscorelibs.ca.cadef.expect_ECA_NORMAL
documentation: For routines which are simply expected to succeed this routine should be
assigned to the routine's errcheck attribute.

functions.name: python.epicscorelibs.ca.cadef.expect_connected
documentation:
sentinel is the disconnected notification value to check for, and chid
is the position in the argument list of the channel being inspected.
Routine for testing functions which interrogate the status of a channel and
return a special sentinel value when the channel is disconnected.

functions.name: python.epicscorelibs.ca.cadef.convert_py_object
documentation: We set things up so that the data type returned by ca_puser() below is a
Python object (to be precise, the argument passed in to ca_create_channel),
but this means that we need to get the reference counting right.
The ctypes library assumes when a routine returns a py_object type that
the called routine has already incremented the object's reference count.
If the object is just a void* then this isn't going to be so: this routine
when assigned to errcheck will resolve this problem.

classes.name: python.epicscorelibs.ca.cadef.event_handler_args

class_variables.name: python.epicscorelibs.ca.cadef.event_handler_args._fields_
documentation: ECA_ status code of operation
Function wrappers.
Wrappers for callbacks from C back into Python.
Event handler for ca_array_get_callback, ca_array_put_callback,
ca_create_subscription.  The event handler is called when the action
completes or the data is available.

classes.name: python.epicscorelibs.ca.cadef.exception_handler_args

class_variables.name: python.epicscorelibs.ca.cadef.exception_handler_args._fields_
documentation: ... and line number
Exception handler, called to report asynchronous errors that have no other
report path.

classes.name: python.epicscorelibs.ca.cadef.ca_connection_handler_args
documentation: Connection handler, used to report channel connection status.

classes.name: python.epicscorelibs.ca.cadef.Disconnected
documentation: The channel is disconnected.
Exception handling support
The following defines exceptions associated with libca errors together
with error handling wrappers to detect the errors and raise the appropriate
exceptions.

classes.name: python.epicscorelibs.ca.cadef.CAException
documentation: Exception in response to calling ca_ method.

modules.name: python.epicscorelibs.config
documentation:
Host configuration information using EPICS names and build flags.

Patterned after sysconfig

modules.name: python.epicscorelibs.ca.dbr
documentation:
Channel access datatype support.  Derived from definitions in the EPICS
header file db_access.h

functions.name: python.epicscorelibs.ca.dbr.copy_attributes_none
documentation: DBR type definitions
All the following types are used to overlay dbr data returned from channel
access or passed into channel access.

functions.name: python.epicscorelibs.ca.dbr._datatype_to_dbr
documentation:
Converts Python datatype into a dbrcode and numpy dtype if possible,
otherwise raises appropriate exception.

functions.name: python.epicscorelibs.ca.dbr._type_to_dbrcode
documentation:
Converts a datatype and format request to a dbr value, or raises an
exception if this cannot be done.

datatype can be either a DBF_XXXX value as returned by ca_field_type() or
data type supported by numpy

format can be one of
  - FORMAT_RAW: retrieve the raw format only
  - FORMAT_TIME: retrieve timestamp and alarm status data
  - FORMAT_CTRL: retrieve limit and control data

functions.name: python.epicscorelibs.ca.dbr._make_strings
documentation: Helper functions for string arrays used in _convert_str_{str,bytes} below.

functions.name: python.epicscorelibs.ca.dbr._convert_char_str
documentation: Conversion functions from raw_dbr to specified format.  These all take a
raw_dbr and compute the appropriate selected Python type.  One of these
conversion functions is selected internally with type_to_dbr below.
Conversion from char array to strings

functions.name: python.epicscorelibs.ca.dbr._convert_char_bytes
documentation: Conversion from char array to bytes strings

functions.name: python.epicscorelibs.ca.dbr._convert_char_unicode
documentation: Conversion from char array to unicode strings

functions.name: python.epicscorelibs.ca.dbr._convert_str_str
documentation: Arrays of standard strings.

functions.name: python.epicscorelibs.ca.dbr._convert_str_bytes
documentation: Arrays of bytes strings.

functions.name: python.epicscorelibs.ca.dbr._convert_str_unicode
documentation: Arrays of unicode strings.

functions.name: python.epicscorelibs.ca.dbr._convert_other
documentation: For everything that isn't a string we either return a scalar or a ca_array

functions.name: python.epicscorelibs.ca.dbr.type_to_dbr
documentation:
Converts data request into the appropriate dbr code and conversion.  The
channel must be ready so that its field type can be interrogated.  Returns
dbr code together with conversion function for transforming dbr values of
that type back into Python values.

functions.name: python.epicscorelibs.ca.dbr._datatype_to_dtype
documentation: Converts any user specified datatype into dbrcode and dtype.
From Python value to DBR encoding, used by caput()

functions.name: python.epicscorelibs.ca.dbr._require_value
documentation:
Use numpy to convert value into specified target type ready for transport
over channel access.

functions.name: python.epicscorelibs.ca.dbr.value_to_dbr
documentation:
Takes an ordinary Python value and converts it into a value in dbr
format suitable for sending to channel access.  Returns the target
datatype and the number of elements together with a pointer to the raw
data and (for lifetime management) the object containing the data.

variables.name: python.epicscorelibs.ca.dbr.MAX_STRING_SIZE
documentation: Size of string type

variables.name: python.epicscorelibs.ca.dbr.MAX_UNITS_SIZE
documentation: Size of units string

variables.name: python.epicscorelibs.ca.dbr.MAX_ENUM_STRING_SIZE

variables.name: python.epicscorelibs.ca.dbr.MAX_ENUM_STATES
documentation: Numer of possible enumeration strings

variables.name: python.epicscorelibs.ca.dbr.EPICS_epoch
documentation: Seconds from 1970 to 1990

variables.name: python.epicscorelibs.ca.dbr.DBR_PUT_ACKT
documentation: Configure global alarm acknowledgement

variables.name: python.epicscorelibs.ca.dbr.DBR_PUT_ACKS
documentation: Acknowledge global alarm

classes.name: python.epicscorelibs.ca.dbr.ca_array
documentation: Augmented array used for all return values with more than one element.

classes.name: python.epicscorelibs.ca.dbr.ca_str

classes.name: python.epicscorelibs.ca.dbr.dbr_string

classes.name: python.epicscorelibs.ca.dbr.dbr_time_string

classes.name: python.epicscorelibs.ca.dbr.dbr_ctrl_short
documentation: DBR types with full control and graphical fields

classes.name: python.epicscorelibs.ca.dbr.InvalidDatatype
